anderson-promoters.name: Anderson promoters
anderson-promoters.description: The Anderson constitutive promoter library (BBa_J231xx): a family of short (35 bp) sigma-70 promoters that share one -35/-10 scaffold and differ at only a few positions, giving a graded series of constitutive strengths from the strong references (J23100, J23119) down to weak members. Widely used as a calibrated set of constitutive expression levels in bacteria.

references.title: Measuring the activity of BioBrick promoters using an in vivo reference standard
references.authors: Kelly JR, Rubin AJ, Davis JH, et al.

phage-att-sites.name: Phage attachment sites for chromosomal integration
phage-att-sites.description: Phage-borne attP attachment sites from phylogenetically distinct, mutually orthogonal site-specific recombination systems, each recognized by its own dedicated integrase and not cross-reacting with the others: phiC31 attP (Streptomyces phage phiC31; a large serine integrase that needs no host factors and gives highly directional integration), and the tyrosine-integrase attP sites of coliphages lambda, 186 (a P2-family system that also uses the Apl directionality factor), phi80, and HK022 (which use the host integration host factor IHF, and an excisionase for the reverse reaction). Each attP is the phage half of an attP x attB recombination pair; the cognate bacterial attB sites (in the host chromosome, or a pseudo-attB in non-native hosts for phiC31) are being added. These orthogonal sites are widely used as landing pads for stable, single-copy genomic integration.
